text: Home is the place that has memorized you — your weight on the floorboards, the hour you reach for water in the dark. It is not architecture but accumulation: of return, of forgiveness, of the particular silence that exists only between people who no longer need to explain themselves. Every exile carries a phantom home inside them, luminous and inaccessible, which suggests that home was never a location but a quality of attention we once received without asking.
